title: Design matrix builds without multiplying risk
description: Use strategy.matrix to fan out across versions/OS while keeping secret use and deploy steps on a single trusted leg.
body: Use when testing across multiple language versions, OSes, or feature flags. Do not replicate a deploy or secret-consuming step across every matrix leg (it multiplies blast radius and rate-limit pressure) and do not let fail-fast hide a single platform regression you care about. Actions: define matrix dimensions with include/exclude for the real combinations, set fail-fast deliberately, and keep publish/deploy in a separate job that depends on the matrix via needs. Validation signals include each leg running the intended combination and only one job holding secrets. Risk boundary: fanning a secret or production action across matrix legs widens exposure; keep it on one gated job.
